model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: This is a very close Round of 32 game, but Alabama gets the slight edge because its elite offense gives it the highest single-game ceiling in the matchup and the overall predictive gap between these teams is small. Texas Tech has a very credible path with the better defensive profile, but the recent injury cloud around JT Toppin makes the Red Raiders a bit harder to trust at full strength against a team that can score in bunches. In a near toss-up, I’d lean to Alabama’s offensive firepower.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced team-quality check with injury context
reasoningStep.summary: Alabama and Texas Tech rate very similarly overall, with Texas Tech holding the better defense and Alabama the better offense. The deciding factor was availability uncertainty for Texas Tech’s star JT Toppin, which slightly shifts a near-even matchup toward Alabama’s higher offensive ceiling.
evidence: Ratings: Alabama is No. 16 KenPom / No. 17 Torvik; Texas Tech is No. 19 KenPom / No. 12 Torvik, so the overall quality gap is very small.
Offense/defense split: Alabama has the No. 3 offense in both KenPom and Torvik but a weaker defense, while Texas Tech is more balanced with a top-12 offense and clearly better defense.

Recent news search surfaced Texas Tech injury concerns: ESPN reported JT Toppin injured his lower right leg late in the season, and Christian Anderson also suffered a muscle strain in the Big 12 tournament.
Texas Tech has a credible upset path by leveraging its stronger defense and more balanced profile, which keeps confidence modest.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.snippet: No other team has suffered the biggest blow ahead of March Madness than the Texas Tech Red Raiders. The team lost its biggest star, JT Toppin, in a knee injury , and it is more than likely that he might not be returning in the tournament. Despite this, Texas Tech has remained valiant but their offensive production has not been the same since.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
